video:video.video:title: Trump declines to say if he and Xi discussed Taiwan
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump arrived at the Temple of Heaven with Chinese President Xi Jinping after the pair discussed trade, Taiwan and other differences in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump offers platitudes while Xi issues warnings on Taiwan at China summit
video:video.video:description: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump started a crucial series of meetings in Beijing on Thursday in a U.S.

video:video.video:title: California Governor candidate Xavier Becerra rallies crowd in campaign event
video:video.video:description: Candidate for California Governor Xavier Becerra held a campaign rally in San Francisco Wednesday night.

video:video.video:title: A musket not a firearm? Tell that to George Washington!
video:video.video:description: If you go to a reenactment this 250th anniversary year, you might be surprised to know that the muskets being fired aren't technically considered firearms in most states.

video:video.video:title: Trump offers platitudes while Xi warns of possible confrontation during China summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has called Chinese President Xi Jinping a friend, but his warm words were in stark contrast to Xi, who opened a bilateral meeting by cautioning about potential conflict between the U.S. and China.

video:video.video:title: Chinese premier meets Elon Musk, Jensen Huang, Tim Cook in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Chinese Premier Li Qiang stressed the need for friendship and cooperation in U.S.-China ties Thursday afternoon as he spoke with U.S. business leaders accompanying President Donald Trump in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: Nicolas Cage goes dark for ‘Spider-Noir’
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Cage reveals the inspirations behind new TV series “Spider-Noir."

video:video.video:title: Florida’s major power company prepares for real storms with annual mock hurricane drill
video:video.video:description: Florida Power & Light Company's mock hurricane drill simulates a response to a Category 2 hurricane named Aiden impacting the Vero Beach, Florida area.

video:video.video:title: Paul Bettany and Will Sharpe revisit 'Amadeus'
video:video.video:description: Will Sharpe plays Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart in new Starz limited series, "Amadeus." Paul Bettany plays his nemesis, Antonio Salieri, whose envy and bitter obsession with Mozart saw him turn against his friend and colleague.

video:video.video:title: Small medical plane crashes in New Mexico, killing all 4 people aboard
video:video.video:description: A small medical plane crashed in a mountain range outside Ruidoso, New Mexico, killing all four people aboard and sparking a wildfire in the surrounding forest.

video:video.video:title: Molten lava spews into the sky as Hawaii's Kilauea Volcano experiences another eruption
video:video.video:description: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano shoots lava into the sky in the latest display of a powerful eruption, shooting molten rock into the surrounding area.

video:video.video:title: Benjamin Franklin House in London recalls the beginnings of US-UK ties
video:video.video:description: Benjamin Franklin is widely known as one of the United States’ Founding Fathers. Less well known is that he spent 16 years living in London. (AP Video by Kwiyeon Ha)

video:video.video:title: Flames and violins fuse in Finland's Eurovision performance
video:video.video:description: Eurovision Song Contest favorites, Finland's Linda Lampenius x Pete Parkkonen discuss getting special permission to play live violin, for their performance of "Flamethrower."

video:video.video:title: Flames and violins fuse in Finland's Eurovision performance
video:video.video:description: Finnish duo of singer Pete Parkkonen and violinist Linda Lampenius bring the heat to Eurovision in an act that involves jets of flame, a valuable 18th-century violin and a team of “ninjas” working to avert disaster.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 9-14 May 2026

video:video.video:title: China issues Taiwan warning as Xi and Trump summit focuses on business links
video:video.video:description: Behind closed doors, Xi told Trump during a meeting that, if Taiwan is handled well, U.S.-China relations “will enjoy overall stability".

video:video.video:title: Trump invites Xi to visit White House in September during banquet toast in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump each proposed a toast during a state dinner at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: US teen Buddhist lama is now a monk studying in the Himalayan foothills
video:video.video:description: Jalue Dorje grew up a typical American teen, loving video games and football. But he's also an aspiring spiritual leader recognized from an early age by the Dalai Lama as a reincarnated lama.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he and Xi discussed Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he discussed Iran with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Trump said the two have similar feelings on Iran that they both don’t want Iran to have a nuclear weapon.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians in Gaza say mass displacement suffered by their predecessors on Nakba Day continues today
video:video.video:description: Israeli bombardment and mass displacement in Gaza have left many Palestinians comparing the current war to the Nakba of 1948, when hundreds of thousands were forced from their homes during the creation of Israel.

video:video.video:title: Lady Gaga arrives at 'Mayhem Requiem' premiere
video:video.video:description: Lady Gaga arrives to screening of “Apple Music Live: Lady Gaga Mayhem Requiem.”

video:video.video:title: Trump leaves Beijing after last meetings with Xi
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump wrapped up his visit to Beijing Friday after a private meeting at China's leader Xi Jinping's official residence and departed for Washington.

video:video.video:title: Lady Gaga: 'You can rewrite your history'
video:video.video:description: Lady Gaga delivers a speech before a screening of “Apple Music Live: Lady Gaga Mayhem Requiem,” a filmed performance capturing her one-night-only show reimagining tracks from her “Mayhem” album.

video:video.video:title: King Charles visits Jewish area of London hit by antisemitic attacks
video:video.video:description: Britain's King Charles III visited Golders Green on Thursday, an area of London which has seen a series of attacks over the past few months that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

video:video.video:title: Tiger bag worn by Musk’s son goes viral in China
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk’s 6-year-old son showed up Thursday at the Great Hall of the People decked in Chinese-style clothing and wearing a tiger bag that has since gone viral in China.

video:video.video:title: Unable to perform, Cuban artist struggles to reinvent himself as crisis deepens
video:video.video:description: Juan Miguel Mas is an avant-garde choreographer and director who founded his own company of plus-size performers in the late 1990s in Cuba.

video:video.video:title: Animal welfare group finds systemic neglect at public dog shelters in Romania
video:video.video:description: An undercover investigation into conditions in Romania’s publicly funded shelters for stray dogs, carried out by the international animal welfare organization Vier Pfoten, known as Four Paws, found what it describes as “high death rates and disturbing conditions” amounting to “systemic neglect.”

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 14
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, May 14th: Trump and Xi Jinping meet in Beijing for talks; Jerusalem braces for march with ultranationalists; Yemen and Houthi rebels agree to detainee swap; Newly restored tombs in Luxor are inaugurated.

video:video.video:title: iPads in schools? AP follows fight against digital devices in a school district
video:video.video:description: Kids in Lower Merion school district in Pennsylvania get iPads starting in kindergarten, switch to Chromebooks in second grade and get their own MacBooks in eighth grade.

video:video.video:title: Chile student protest escalates as police clash with demonstrators in Santiago
video:video.video:description: Thousands of students marched through downtown Santiago on Thursday to protest education cuts and rising living costs, in one of the largest student demonstrations in recent months.

video:video.video:title: 'Dirtnado' sweeps through Minnesota farm
video:video.video:description: A "dirtnado" was captured sweeping through a northern Montana farm.

video:video.video:title: Man accused of wounding 2 drivers in Cambridge shooting pleads not guilty from hospital bed
video:video.video:description: A man accused of firing at least 70 rounds from a rifle on a busy street and wounding two drivers near Boston has pleaded not guilty to assault and other charges when he was arraigned from his hospital bed.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine’s Leléka wants to reach you
video:video.video:description: Ukraine’s Eurovision star Leléka explains why she’s breaking with tradition and singing in both English and Ukrainian at the annual song contest. (May 14)

video:video.video:title: Two newly-restored tombs inaugurated in the West Bank of Luxor
video:video.video:description: Ancient wall paintings and inscriptions inside the Luxor tombs have been restored after years of conservation work.

video:video.video:title: Barbara Palvin shows off baby bump with Dylan Sprouse in Cannes
video:video.video:description: Actor Dylan Sprouse and his model wife Barbara Palvin are expecting their first baby. The couple announced the news at the 79th Annual Cannes Film Festival. (May 14)

video:video.video:title: The Beach Boys on 60 years of 'Pet Sounds' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: The surviving members of The Beach Boys — Mike Love, Al Jardine and Bruce Johnston — reunited at the iconic Capitol Records building in Los Angeles to celebrate the 60th anniversary of "Pet Sounds," the landmark album that was released May 16, 1966.

video:video.video:title: Stars celebrate women in cinema at annual gala
video:video.video:description: Demi Moore, Diane Kruger and Rami Malek are a few of the stars who attended the Red Sea Film Foundation's 2026 "Women In Cinema" Gala. (May 14)

video:video.video:title: 14-year-old boy among 6 dead from Honduras and Mexico found in hot Texas shipping container
video:video.video:description: More details are out after six people were found dead in a rail yard shipping container in Laredo, Texas, last weekend.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana senators pass new US House redistricting map
video:video.video:description: State senators in Louisiana passed a new congressional map that would eliminate a majority-Black district after the U.S.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: World Cup pitch at Dallas stadium unveiled
video:video.video:description: Officials on Wednesday unveiled the newly installed World Cup pitch inside Dallas Stadium in Arlington, Texas where suspended LED grow lights are being used to help maintain the grass field ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup matches.

video:video.video:title: Xi says U.S.-China relationship is most important in the world
video:video.video:description: China’s leader Xi Jinping called for the United States and Beijing to work together as “partners” rather than "rivals" in an opening toast ahead of a state banquet that was largely positive, though measured.

video:video.video:title: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says US policy toward Taiwan 'unchanged'
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said U.S. policy toward Taiwan was “unchanged” but warned that it would be “a terrible mistake” for China to take Taiwan by force.

video:video.video:title: Trump invites Xi to the White House during state dinner in Beijing
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump extended a Sept. 24 invitation to Chinese President Xi Jinping and his wife, Madame Peng, during his reciprocal toast at a state dinner in the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: Families scour southern Mexico after 40 migrants vanished on a dangerous sea route
video:video.video:description: Relatives of dozens of missing migrants travelled to Mexico’s southern Pacific coast to search for clues about family members who disappeared while attempting a maritime journey toward the United States.

video:video.video:title: Ultra-nationalist Jews arrive in Jerusalem's Old City for annual march
video:video.video:description: Jerusalem braced for tens of thousands of ultranationalist Jews to parade through Palestinian areas of the Old City on Thursday during an annual march that is often characterized by racist chants and violence.

video:video.video:title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces Labour Party leadership challenge
video:video.video:description: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting quit embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Cabinet Thursday in what is expected to be a precursor to challenging his leadership.

video:video.video:title: Former Oklahoma death row prisoner freed from jail as he awaits retrial in 1997 killing
video:video.video:description: Richard Glossip was released from incarceration for the first time in nearly 30 years Thursday after posting bond while awaiting retrial for a 1997 killing that put him on the brink of execution three separate times.

video:video.video:title: Miners use dynamite in protest demanding a labor reform
video:video.video:description: Clashes erupted Thursday in Bolivia's capital as police used tear gas to disperse a crowd of miners trying to breach the government palace and setting off small dynamite charges, a tactic that has become increasingly common during this second week of nationwide unrest.

video:video.video:title: Russia hammers Ukraine for a 3rd straight day, flattening a Kyiv apartment block
video:video.video:description: Russia on Thursday unleashed a third straight day of massive drone and missile attacks on Ukraine, demolishing an apartment building in Kyiv where nine people were killed and dozens injured, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Indiana woman formerly prescribed Mifepristone reacts to Supreme Court abortion pill decision
video:video.video:description: For Kristina Moorhead, a woman in Indiana who was prescribed the pills twice to complete two missed miscarriages in 2024, the decision “is incredibly important”.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: King Charles III meets guests and enters the DJ booth at Buckingham Palace garden party
video:video.video:description: Briain's King Charles III held a party at Buckingham Palace gardens on Thursday to celebrate 50 years since the launch of his charity.

video:video.video:title: Latvian prime minister resigns after controversy over stray Ukrainian drones
video:video.video:description: Latvian center-right Prime Minister Evika Silina announced her resignation on Thursday, after the Progressives Party, her left-leaning coalition partner, pulled support from the government and left her without a majority.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo meets students just evacuated from Gaza at Rome university
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV was warmly welcomed by students at Europe’s largest university on Thursday including young Palestinians who arrived in Italy this week from Gaza to continue their studies.

video:video.video:title: Iran war energy shock raises Asia's interest in fuel blending
video:video.video:description: India is pushing for the use of more biofuels in transportation in order to slash dependence on imported fossil fuels, which have been disrupted by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Iran FM: Strait of Hormuz suffering 'first and most from US blockade'
video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi criticized the U.S. in a televised interview on Thursday, blaming tensions in the Strait of Hormuz on "U.S. aggression".

video:video.video:title: Princess of Wales visits Italy after announcing her cancer was in remission
video:video.video:description: Britain’s Princess Catherine made her first overseas trip since announcing her cancer was in remission.

video:video.video:title: BRICS foreign ministers meet in India as Iran war, oil prices and divisions test the bloc's unity
video:video.video:description: Foreign ministers from the BRICS nations began a two-day meeting in New Delhi on Thursday as the expanding bloc faces divisions over the war in Iran, rising energy prices and growing global economic uncertainty.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 14
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, May 14TH: Chinese President Xi welcomes Trump to Beijing; Russia launches massive missile and drone attack on Kyiv, Ukraine; UAE denies that Israeli PM Netanyahu visited; Woman who wrote children’s book on grief sentenced for husband’s murder.

video:video.video:title: Keke Palmer brings the good vibes
video:video.video:description: At the Los Angeles premiere of comedy "I Love Boosters," Keke Palmer describes her philosophy of life.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of Russian strikes on Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles
video:video.video:description: Russia targeted Ukraine's capital with a mass drone and missile attack early Thursday morning that killed at least one and injured 33 people, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers search for people under the rubble after Russian attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Russia attacked Ukraine’s capital with a mass drone and missile strike early Thursday morning. The attack killed at least one and injured 33 people, according to the local authorities.

video:video.video:title: Trump's '51st US state' talk met with defiance in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: Twice in as many days this week, U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed interest in turning Venezuela into his country’s 51st state.

video:video.video:title: 'Fast & Furious' cast reunite at Cannes Film Festival
video:video.video:description: Vin Diesel, Meadow Walker and the 'Fast & Furious' stars celebrated the 25th anniversary of the franchise with a special midnight screening.

video:video.video:title: Muskets from the American Revolutionary era are mostly exempt from gun laws in the US
video:video.video:description: A musket from 1776 can fire a lead ball at a velocity of around 1,000 feet per second. Imagine what that can do to a human body

video:video.video:title: Lady Gaga unleashes 'MAYHEM' in LA
video:video.video:description: On Thursday, Lady Gaga made a dramatic entrance at the premiere of “Lady Gaga: Apple Music Live (MAYHEM Requiem),” a special capturing her one-night-only Los Angeles performance earlier this year that reimagined songs from her Grammy-winning album “Mayhem.”

video:video.video:title: Russia and Ukraine exchange prisoners, with each side releasing 205 detainees
video:video.video:description: Russia’s Defense Ministry said that Russia and Ukraine had a prisoner exchange on Thursday, each side releasing 205.

video:video.video:title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping meet over tea in Zhongnanhai
video:video.video:description: Xi Jinping welcomes Donald Trump at his official residence, Zhongnanhai, on Friday for the final talks of the summit.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 15
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, May 15th: Trump concludes summit with Chinese President Xi; Ship seized off UAE, taken to Iran; Zelenskyy says Russia has fired over 1,500 drones at Ukraine; Plane crashes into home in Akron, Ohio.

video:video.video:title: Affordable art fair in Hong Kong boasts live calligraphy painting and Brit art
video:video.video:description: Live calligraphy from Asia, modern Brit art and ships inside glass whales have all been curated for Hong Kong's Affordable Art Fair.

video:video.video:title: Cannes and the Hôtel Martinez await a 'White Lotus' close-up
video:video.video:description: A look at The Hôtel Martinez, one of the iconic luxury hotels that line the beach in Cannes, which will be a central location for Season 4 of hit TV series, "The White Lotus."

video:video.video:title: US-China summit ends with Xi and Trump claiming progress but differences remain
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump wrapped up his visit to Beijing Friday, flying back to Washington after a a private meeting at Chinese leader Xi Jinping's official residence.

video:video.video:title: Boxing becomes an outlet for girls in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Under the blazing sun in Gaza’s Muwasi area of Khan Younis, girls of different ages line up between rows of tents to practice boxing, throwing punches at worn punching bags and makeshift training pads fashioned from plastic poles and padded tarps.

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore, Lucas Bravo among guests at Women in Cinema Gala
video:video.video:description: The Red Sea Film Foundation's annual gala in Cap d'Antibes, South of France, honored Laïla Marrakchi, Genevieve Nnaji, Tara Sutaria, Marie-Clementine Dusabejambo, Kamila Andani and Aixa Kay.

video:video.video:title: NVIDIA chief Jensen Huang eats noodles on streets of Beijing
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted on Friday trying some of Beijing’s delicacies. He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.

video:video.video:title: Experts warn motorists not to run down gas tanks too low as prices spike, Memorial Day approaches
video:video.video:description: Experts are warning drivers to be careful not to run down their gas tanks too low as prices continue to spike and the travel-heavy Memorial Day holiday approaches (AP video: Mike Householder

video:video.video:title: India raises fuel prices as global energy crisis puts pressure on economy
video:video.video:description: India raised fuel prices by 3 rupees ($0.03) per liter Friday as the government moved to offset losses due to higher global oil prices.

video:video.video:title: 2 people found dead inside a plane that crashed into an Ohio home
video:video.video:description: Fire officials say two people were found dead in a plane after it crashed into a house in Ohio and exploded into flames.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Steve Cohen ending campaign in Tennessee after redraw of his Memphis district
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Steven Cohen of Tennessee ends his bid for reelection, his career upended by the redistricting battles that are sweeping the country after last month’s Supreme Court decision.

video:video.video:title: Emergency services battle fire following latest attack on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: One person was killed in the latest attack on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia on Friday, according to State Emergency Service of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Jerome Powell's tenure as Fed chair marked by fight for independence while trying to tame inflation
video:video.video:description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell finishes his eight-year term on Friday, leaving behind a complicated legacy as he battled inflation and fought with President Donald Trump to protect the Fed's independence.

video:video.video:title: The lighting technology that brings the Eurovision Song Contest staging alive
video:video.video:description: The countdown is underway to the Eurovision Song Contest grand final. The 70th edition of the event, being held this year in Vienna, is one of the most technically ambitious ever.

video:video.video:title: John Travolta dons beret in Cannes
video:video.video:description: In Cannes, John Travolta walks the carpet with the cast of his directorial debut, "Propeller One-way Night Coach," which is screening in the festival's Cannes Premiere section.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he'll 'be making decisions' on Taiwan arms package after Xi summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he has not yet decided whether a major sale of US arms to Taiwan can move forward following his three-day visit to China.

video:video.video:title: Trump said he didn’t ask China's Xi ‘for any favors’ on Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday he did not ask Chinese President Xi Jinping “for any favors” on Iran during their Beijing summit, insisting the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Congressmen on both sides support arms sale to Taiwan despite Trump's hesitation
video:video.video:description: Republican and Democratic member of Congress say they support another arms sale to Taiwan and the U.S. defending the island Democracy, despite U.S.

video:video.video:title: Kyiv mourns as death toll from Russian attack in Ukrainian capital rises to 24
video:video.video:description: The death toll from a Russian missile attack that flattened a Kyiv apartment building rose to 24, officials said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Indonesia's first giant panda cub Rio growing and healthy before his public debut
video:video.video:description: The first giant panda born in Indonesia squirmed as veterinarians checked his hearing and eyesight and confirmed the furry cub was growing and healthy Friday before his public debut later this month.

video:video.video:title: Shakira explains how her 2010 World Cup anthem 'Waka Waka' made her a mother
video:video.video:description: Shakira reminisces about her 2010 FIFA World Cup anthem "Waka Waka (This Time For Africa)," saying the song turned her into a mother. (May 15)

video:video.video:title: Medical plane crashes and sparks fire in New Mexico mountain range
video:video.video:description: A small medical plane crashed in a mountain range outside Ruidoso, New Mexico, before dawn Thursday, killing all four people aboard and sparking a wildfire in the surrounding forest, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment a cuttlefish changes color
video:video.video:description: Underwater footage captures the moment a cuttlefish changed colors from brown to a bright yellow off the coast of Sydney.

video:video.video:title: Paul McCartney and Wings members' artifacts on display at exhibit at Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
video:video.video:description: The largest collection of Paul McCartney's personal artifacts ever publicly displayed is part of a new exhibit at the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in Cleveland, centering on his life after the Beatles and his band Wings.

video:video.video:title: Owl found in concrete mixer is on the mend and maybe a bit wiser from ordeal
video:video.video:description: An adolescent owl that was found stuck in a concrete mixer in southwestern Utah is finally on the mend, flying free and maybe a bit wiser from the ordeal.

video:video.video:title: A political dynasty heiress and a former trade minister advance to Peru’s presidential runoff, final vote tally shows
video:video.video:description: Conservative Keiko Fujimori and progressive Roberto Sánchez will compete for Peru’s presidency in a June 7 runoff election, according to the final vote count from an election held more than a month ago.

video:video.video:title: A mosque and two vehicles are vandalized in the occupied West Bank as settler's violence intensifies
video:video.video:description: A mosque and two vehicles were vandalized in the occupied West Bank village of Jibiya early Friday, according to residents and the Palestinian Ministry of Religious Affairs, as settler's violence intensifies in the Palestinian territory.

video:video.video:title: Trump still mulling US arms sale to Taiwan
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he’s not yet made a determination on whether a major U.S. sale of arms to Taiwan can move forward, following his three-day visit to China.

video:video.video:title: What to know about new Ebola outbreak that has killed scores of people in Congo
video:video.video:description: Africa’s top public health body has confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in Congo's Ituri province. There have been 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths so far.

video:video.video:title: Judge declares a mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s rape retrial after jury deadlocks
video:video.video:description: Jurors deadlocked in Harvey Weinstein’s rape retrial Friday, forcing another mistrial in a #MeToo-era case that has gone to trial three times so far.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano erupts again
video:video.video:description: Kilauea has periodically sent fountains of lava soaring during more than a year of an on-and-off eruption.

video:video.video:title: Maine lumber mill explosion injures at least 11 as firefighters respond to roaring blaze
video:video.video:description: A large fire and explosion at a lumber mill in Maine's midcoast region injured at least 11 people Friday.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike targets Hamas leader in Gaza
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday targeted the leader of Hamas’ military wing, Israeli officials say, but it wasn't immediately clear if Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed or injured.

video:video.video:title: North America’s largest commuter rail system faces a potential shutdown
video:video.video:description: North America’s largest commuter rail system with 250,000 daily riders faces a shutdown if a deal isn't reached with unionized workers to avoid a strike.

video:video.video:title: Israel and Lebanon agree to 45-day extension of ceasefire, US says
video:video.video:description: US officials says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to 45-day extension of ceasefire. It comes after two “productive” days of talks in Washington and will be followed by more negotiations June 2-3, said State Department spokesman.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang eats noodles on the streets of Beijing
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted trying some of Beijing’s delicacies. He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 15
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, May 15th: Trump says he's undecided on sending weapons to Taiwan; Iran's FM says lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the U.S.; Pentagon withdraws U.S. troops from Europe; Judge declares mistrial in Harvey Weinstein's rape retrial.

video:video.video:title: Tennessee man known for posting racist videos gets $1.25 million bond on attempted murder charge
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee man who goes by Chud the Builder and is known for posting racist videos has been given a $1.25 million preliminary bond on attempted murder charges.

video:video.video:title: Researchers unearth Southeast Asia's largest dinosaur
video:video.video:description: A giant new species of dinosaur discovered in Thailand is thought to be the biggest ever found in Southeast Asia.

video:video.video:title: Maine lumber mill explosion injures at least 11 as firefighters respond to roaring blaze
video:video.video:description: A large fire and explosion at a lumber mill in Maine’s midcoast region has injured at least 11 people.

video:video.video:title: Power outages and fuel shortages deepen frustrations across Cuba
video:video.video:description: Residents in Havana banged pots and pans overnight Thursday into Friday as prolonged power outages and fuel shortages deepened frustrations across Cuba.

video:video.video:title: 'Is God Is' writer and director Aleshea Harris on how the film explores unapologetic female rage
video:video.video:description: Award-winning playwright Aleshea Harris is bringing "Is God Is" to the screen. The film follows two twin sisters, Racine and Anaia, who embark on a quest for revenge to confront their dark family history.

video:video.video:title: President Trump returns to White House; says bilateral trip to China was a 'tremendous success'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump called his three-day trip to China a 'tremendous success' after meeting President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Beef consumption in Argentina falls to lowest level in 20 years
video:video.video:description: Red meat consumption in Argentina — historically one of the world’s biggest consumers of beef — has fallen to its lowest level in two decades. (AP/ Cristian Kovadloff)

video:video.video:title: Connor Swindells calls Odessa A'zion the 'coolest person I've ever met'
video:video.video:description: Connor Swindells and Odessa A'zion were honored at Chopard’s Cannes party, where Swindells gushed over A’zion, calling her “the coolest person I’ve ever met.” (May 15)

video:video.video:title: Eurovision historian says this year's edition has 'been rather festive'
video:video.video:description: Dean Vuletic, who has been dubbed "Professor Eurovision," discusses protests against Israel’s participation in the annual song contest.

video:video.video:title: A handful of women who never heard of handball now are training to join the US Olympic team
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of women have tried out for a chance to join the U.S. women’s handball team for the 2028 Olympics.

video:video.video:title: A Latino grocery store in Delaware turns its produce aisle into a music stage
video:video.video:description: A family-owned Latino grocery store in Delaware is attracting online audiences and bands from far away with monthly concerts from diverse artists and genres.

video:video.video:title: Lasers and robots: US farmers turning to automation amid labor shortages and aging workforce
video:video.video:description: As the U.S. agricultural workforce shrinks and ages, farmers are increasingly adopting artificial intelligence and robotic systems to manage everything from weed control to dairy production.
